item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To clarify a few things.

I didn't think I needed to specify but the cave entrance is large enough for a -O instance to exit, so there's plenty of room. This is why theres a guard both inside and outside the cave. Similarly both in and out of the cave is salt water.

As far as what the fish people eat, the Foundation don't know. In my headcanon they're cannibalistic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, though I wonder if they've genetically tested any SCP-3229-A to determine if they're even genetically human? I'd think they would've tested the D-Class who turned into one.

Are there any other organisms besides SCP-3229-A, -B, and -O? What do the fish people eat? Is the water inside the caves equivalent to the ocean water outside?

A little info on how they translated the writing (which is presumably unrelated to any known writing system/language) would be nice.

Can they fit boats/subs through the cave entrance or did they have to assemble them piece by piece inside?

commonly 2-4 inches in diameter

This should be in cm.

Also, I think the "humanoid" tag applies to this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I mean, the architecture is probably non-euclidean. At least it's accurate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Best example: the Earth is Non Euclidean.

Walk 10,000 km. Turn 90 degrees left. Walk another 10,000 km. Turn 90 degrees left. Walk 10,000 km once more. Arrive back where you started. If everything took place on a euclidean plane, this would be impossible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The majority of islands inside SCP-3229 are covered in buildings of non-euclidean architecture.

Yeah, welcome to Earth. I think it's pretty clear you were just kind of copying Lovecraft and the terms he used… FYI, "Non Euclidean" means "Math that takes place on a non-flat plane", AKA anything in real life.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh it's totally allowed. Lots of site authors promote their own work in the discussion thread. Some even link them at the end of their own article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm very glad you enjoyed it! I'd bump my other lovecraftian skip but i'm not sure if that's allowed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now this is how you do Lovecraft fiction!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Noting here that this article was tagged incorrectly with the nonexistent "parasite" tag. Changed it to the correct "parasitic" tag.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here’s a hyperlink to the image source: https://commons.m.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Limestone_Pakarang_Cave_Ceiling.JPG

As for the skip itself, I like it. It’s pretty well fleshed out, and I definitely got the chills at some points. This appealed to me in the same way and somewhat reminds me of SCP-3391— maybe they’re related?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Source for the image: File:Limestone Pakarang Cave Ceiling.JPG
From Wikimedia Commons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks mate, hope you catch the proper Lovecraft references in there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mhmhm, more please.

Got some shadows over innsmouth vibes, given the fishmen and "voluntary" transformation into them. Not quite the eldritch horror, but this is some nice groundwork for an exploration tale maybe.
Either way, solid SCP, hope you write more about this.
+1
